_meta.status: CCB-S3-001 — copy ported from the operator's approved website template, revised per operator: no em dashes in visible copy, expanded text. Legal placeholder fields (street, dates …) remain to be filled; Privacy/Terms are drafts pending the planning chat's final texts.

brand.name: Cinderella
brand.tagline: The AI bot suite for SimpleX and Matrix communities
lang.label: Language
hero.ann: Now in alpha · consent + CSAM screening
hero.lede: Cinderella is a firewall in front of the public archive: every message and file passes through consent checks and CSAM screening before anything ever goes live. What fails a check never leaves the pipeline, and every decision is written to the audit log.
home.live.title: Search a permanent, public record
home.live.lede: Full-text search, filters, and media behind authentication. This interactive preview runs entirely in your browser with sample data: type a query, switch groups, and watch the archive respond exactly like the real thing.
home.how.title: Screened before it's seen
home.how.lede: Everything flows through Cinderella first: consent, then content screening, then the public archive. Each stage can stop a message, and nothing skips the queue.
home.tile.csam.title: CSAM screening
home.tile.csam.body: Every image and video is checked against known-abuse databases before it can ever reach the archive. Flagged material is stopped at the door, not cleaned up afterwards.
home.tile.consent.title: Consent-first
home.tile.consent.body: Nothing passes through without the group's explicit opt-in. Consent is checked before capture, never assumed, and can be withdrawn at any time.
home.tile.secure.title: Secure by design
home.tile.secure.body: Passwordless passkeys, encrypted transport, media behind authentication, full audit logging. Security is the foundation, not an add-on.
home.tile.archived.title: Then archived
home.tile.archived.body: Only what clears screening becomes a permanent, searchable public record. Everything else simply never appears.
home.suite.title: The archive is the first capability
home.suite.lede: Cinderella is a growing suite of SimpleX and Matrix bots built around one identity. The public archive is live today, and more capabilities are on the roadmap.
home.suite.archive.title: Public web archive
home.suite.archive.body: Consent-gated capture, full-text search, filters, infinite scroll, video playback, and SEO-friendly public pages. Built to make a community's shared knowledge findable for years, not buried in chat history.
home.suite.roadmap.title: On the roadmap
home.sec.title: Your data stays yours
home.sec.lede: Passwordless passkeys, encrypted transport, and AI that runs locally on your hardware. Nothing leaves your infrastructure, and no third-party cloud ever sees your community's conversations.
demo.consent: Every group opted in. Withdraw anytime.
demo.search.label: Search the archive
features.lede: A firewall pipeline: consent, then CSAM and content screening, and only then the public archive. Every stage is enforced in code, not in policy documents. Here is what each one does.
features.cap1.title: Consent-gated capture
features.cap1.body: The bot joins a public group and asks before anything is read. Capture starts only after an explicit opt-in, and stops the moment consent is withdrawn. Every opt-in and opt-out is recorded, so the archive can always prove why a message is there.
features.cap2.title: CSAM & content screening
features.cap2.body: The heart of the firewall: every image and video is hashed and checked against known-abuse databases before it can be stored. Flagged media is blocked and never reaches the archive. The operator sees it in a review queue, nowhere else.
features.cap3.title: The public web archive
features.cap3.body: Only what clears screening is published. What remains becomes permanent public pages: full-text search across every message, filters by group, author, date and media, infinite scroll, inline video, and SEO-friendly stable URLs. Search engines index it, people actually find it.
features.cap4.title: Reporting & moderation
features.cap4.body: A second line after screening: anyone can report a published message. Reports reach the operator's console for review, takedown, and audit. Moderation is a first-class workflow, not an afterthought.
features.roadmap.title: Planned capabilities
features.roadmap.lede: In development or design. Order and scope may change, because this is alpha software and we would rather ship the safeguards first.
features.rm.matrix.body: The same consent-first archive and bot suite for Matrix rooms.
features.rm.categorization.body: Automatic topic grouping across archived content.
features.rm.videogallery.body: A dedicated browsing surface for archived video and images.
features.rm.moderation.body: Bulk actions, policies, and reviewer queues.
features.rm.localai.body: Screening and summaries that run on your own hardware.
pro.title: Cinderella Pro
pro.lede: Managed hosting and capabilities beyond the open core, for operators who want the archive without running the infrastructure. Same safeguards, same open foundation, none of the server work.
pro.tile1.title: Managed hosting
pro.tile1.body: We run the bots, the archive, and the updates. You keep operational control and your data, and you never touch a server.
pro.tile2.title: Beyond the open core
pro.tile2.body: Pro-only capabilities land here first: advanced moderation, analytics, and multi-archive management. The open core stays complete on its own.
pro.tile3.title: Support that answers
pro.tile3.body: Direct channel to the people who build Cinderella, with response targets. No ticket black holes.
pro.pricing.title: Plans
pro.pricing.lede: Pricing is being finalized during the alpha. The figures below are placeholders, and early interest helps us shape the tiers.
pro.tier1.name: Self-host
pro.tier1.desc: The full open core, AGPL-3.0. Your hardware, your rules.
pro.tier2.name: Pro
pro.tier2.desc: Managed hosting plus Pro capabilities, run for you.
pro.tier3.name: Enterprise
pro.tier3.desc: Multiple archives, custom terms, and deployment assistance.
pro.customer.title: Already a customer?
pro.customer.body: Sign in to your Pro workspace, or leave an address and we'll write when onboarding opens. We onboard in small batches during the alpha.
security.lede: Cinderella is a screening firewall: every message and file runs through consent and CSAM detection before it can be published. This page explains how that is built, and what happens when a check fails.
security.csam.title: CSAM & content screening
security.csam.body: This is the point of Cinderella. Every image and video is hashed and matched against known-abuse databases before it can be stored. Flagged media is blocked at the firewall and never reaches the public archive. It surfaces only in the operator's review queue, where it can be reported and preserved for the authorities.
security.tile1.title: Passwordless
security.tile1.body: Passkeys (WebAuthn / FIDO2): device-bound, phishing- and brute-force-resistant. No shared passwords, nothing to leak or reuse.
security.tile2.title: Your data stays yours
security.tile2.body: Least-privilege and isolated. With the planned local AI, screening and conversations never leave your infrastructure, and there is no third-party processor to trust.
security.tile3.title: Reporting & takedown
security.tile3.body: A second line after screening: anyone can report published content. The operator reviews and removes it, and every action is audit-logged.
security.vuln.title: Found a vulnerability?
security.vuln.body: Report it privately via the security contact in the repository. We credit responsible disclosure and take every report seriously.
os.title: Open core, open books
os.lede: Cinderella's core is free software under AGPL-3.0. Run it yourself, read every line, and hold us to what this site claims. Trust should be verifiable, not promised.
os.repo.title: The repository
os.repo.body: Source, issues, roadmap discussions, and the security policy live on GitHub. Contributions are welcome, from typo fixes to whole capabilities, and every change lands in the open.
os.why.title: Why AGPL-3.0
os.why.body: An archive asks for trust. The AGPL guarantees that anyone operating a modified Cinderella must publish those modifications, so the public can always inspect the code that handles public conversations. That guarantee is the license doing its job.
legal.title: Legal information
legal.lede: Placeholder texts, to be reviewed by counsel before launch. Highlighted fields must be completed.
impressum.title: Legal Notice (Impressum)
impressum.intro: Information in accordance with § 5 TMG / § 18 MStV.
impressum.dispute.body: We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.
impressum.ypo.intro: Cinderella has voluntarily appointed a Youth Protection Officer:
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains what data this site and the Cinderella archive process, why, and for how long.
privacy.s1.ph: Operator identity, same as Legal Notice
privacy.s2.a: The archive stores messages from public SimpleX groups that have explicitly consented to capture. Legal basis:
privacy.s2.b: Content can be reported and removed via the reporting workflow.
privacy.s3.body: The public site sets a session cookie for operator login and counts anonymous usage. No tracking pixels, no third-party advertising, no cross-site profiles.
privacy.s4.ph: Hosting provider / location; note that AI processing runs locally
privacy.s5.body: Access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, objection: contact the operator. You may lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority.
terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.s1.body: These terms govern use of the public archive and the operator console. The software itself is licensed separately under AGPL-3.0.
terms.s2.body: The service is provided in an early alpha state, without warranty of availability or fitness. Features may change or be removed.
terms.s3.body: No scraping beyond published interfaces, no attempts to access non-public data, no abuse of the reporting system.
terms.s4.body: Archived content originates from consenting public groups. The operator may remove content following reports or legal obligation.
terms.s5.ph: Liability clause to be provided by counsel
footer.blurb: The AI bot suite for SimpleX and Matrix communities. Consent-first, permanent, searchable, and open source under AGPL-3.0.
footer.copyright: © 2026 Sascha Dämgen IT and More Systems
footer.built: Built on SimpleX and Matrix. Not affiliated with SimpleX Chat or the Matrix Foundation.

cookie.text: This site uses a session cookie for login and anonymous usage counts. Details in the
